content:
Assignment Title: Critical Analysis of Social Media and Society
Course: Sociology 201 – Contemporary Social Issues
Instructor: Prof. Jane Doe
Due Date: October 10, 2025

Format: Typed, double-spaced, 12-point Times New Roman, APA citation style

Instructions

  • Answer all questions.

  • Use scholarly sources (minimum of three).

  • Support all arguments with evidence.

  • Submit the assignment as a PDF on the university portal.

Questions

1. (5 points)

Define digital divide and explain why it matters in discussions about social media use.

2. (5 points)

Which of the following is an example of the digital divide?

a) Using social media for activism
b) Students in rural areas lacking access to high-speed internet
c) Posting daily on Instagram
d) Using hashtags during protests

3. (15 points)

Evaluate how social media platforms influence political engagement among young adults in the United States.
Your answer should discuss at least two positive impacts and two negative impacts of social media on political engagement.

4.  (20 points)

Read the scenario below:

“During the 2020 U.S. election, misinformation spread widely on Facebook and Twitter. Some users reported feeling overwhelmed and distrustful of news sources.”

Based on this scenario:

  • Identify two ways misinformation can affect democracy.

  • Propose at least two strategies that social media companies or governments could use to reduce misinformation.

5. (30 points)

Write a 3–4 page research paper analyzing the role of social media algorithms in shaping public opinion.
Your paper should include the following:

  • A simplified explanation of how social media algorithms work

  • At least two real-world examples of algorithmic influence (e.g., TikTok, YouTube, Facebook)

  • Ethical implications of algorithm-driven content

  • Your own critical perspective on whether stricter regulation of social media algorithms is necessary
